docs.title: molgenis-flwr-armadillo
docs.text:
A wrapper for running Flower federated learning with MOLGENIS Armadillo.

The open-source edition of Flower doesn't include individual-level authentication. This package extends Flower by providing CLI tools to authenticate with Armadillo servers and run Flower jobs with tokens automatically injected.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
pip install molgenis-flwr-armadillo

Or from source:

pip install git+https://github.com/molgenis/molgenis-flwr-armadillo.git

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
# 1. Authenticate to all Armadillo servers (opens browser for each)
armadillo-flwr-authenticate --config flower-nodes.yaml

# 2. Run the Flower app with tokens automatically injected
armadillo-flwr-run .

docs.title: CLI Tools
docs.text: Command Description armadillo-flwr-authenticate Authenticate to Armadillo servers via OIDC armadillo-flwr-run Run a Flower app with tokens injected armadillo-flwr-resources List accessible projects and resources

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Python 3.9+
  • Access to one or more MOLGENIS Armadillo servers
  • A Flower SuperLink and SuperNode deployment

docs.title: 1. Create flower-nodes.yaml
docs.text:
List the Armadillo servers you want to authenticate to:

urls:
  - "https://armadillo-demo.molgenis.net"
  - "https://armadillo.dev.molgenis.org"

docs.title: 2. Add token placeholders to pyproject.toml
docs.text:
Each URL is sanitized into a config key. Add empty token placeholders:

[tool.flwr.app.config]
token-armadillo-demo-molgenis-net = ""
token-armadillo-dev-molgenis-org = ""

You can check the sanitized key for any URL:

from molgenis_flwr_armadillo import sanitize_url
print(sanitize_url("https://armadillo-demo.molgenis.net"))
# armadillo-demo-molgenis-net

docs.title: 3. Authenticate
docs.text:
armadillo-flwr-authenticate --config flower-nodes.yaml

This opens a browser for OIDC authentication to each server and saves tokens locally.

docs.title: 4. Run your Flower app
docs.text:
armadillo-flwr-run .

Tokens are automatically injected into flwr run --run-config.

docs.title: Server app
docs.text:
The server collects tokens from the run config and forwards them to clients:

from molgenis_flwr_armadillo import extract_tokens

@app.main()
def main(grid: Grid, context: Context) -> None:
    tokens = extract_tokens(context)
    train_config = {"lr": 0.01, **tokens}
    # pass train_config to strategy...

docs.title: Client app
docs.text:
Each client reads its URL and token from the environment / config:

from molgenis_flwr_armadillo import get_node_token, get_node_url, load_data

@app.train()
def train(msg: Message, context: Context):
    url = get_node_url()
    token = get_node_token(msg)
    raw = load_data(url, token, "my-project", "train.parquet")
    df = pd.read_parquet(io.BytesIO(raw))
    # train model...

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Data loading follows the same pattern as DataSHIELD's assign.table(): the client requests data, Armadillo pushes it into the container via Docker, and the helper reads it into memory and deletes the file immediately.

docs.title: The load_data() Function
docs.text:
from molgenis_flwr_armadillo import get_node_token, get_node_url, load_data

url = get_node_url()
token = get_node_token(msg)
raw = load_data(url, token, "my-project", "train.parquet")

docs.title: What happens under the hood
docs.text:
1. load_data() calls POST /flower/push-data on Armadillo
  2. Armadillo validates the OIDC token and checks project permissions
  3. Armadillo reads the resource from storage
  4. Armadillo copies the data into the container via Docker API
  5. load_data() waits for the file to appear at /tmp/armadillo_data/
  6. The file is read into memory as raw bytes
  7. The file is deleted immediately
  8. Raw bytes are returned

docs.title: File lifecycle
docs.text:
Write     Read    Delete     Parse
    |         |        |         |
    └────┬────┴───┬────┘         └── Data in memory only
     File exists  |
                  └── File gone before researcher code runs

docs.title: Parsing the data
docs.text:
load_data() returns raw bytes. Parse with whatever library you need:

import io
import pandas as pd

# Parquet
raw = load_data(url, token, "project", "data.parquet")
df = pd.read_parquet(io.BytesIO(raw))

# CSV
raw = load_data(url, token, "project", "data.csv")
df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(raw))

# PyTorch tensor
import torch
raw = load_data(url, token, "project", "model.pt")
data = torch.load(io.BytesIO(raw))

docs.title: Checking access before training
docs.text:
Use check_access() to verify permissions before starting a long training run:

from molgenis_flwr_armadillo import check_access

check_access(url, token, "my-project", ["train.parquet", "test.parquet"])
# Raises RuntimeError if user lacks access or resources don't exist

docs.title: Listing available data
docs.text:
from molgenis_flwr_armadillo import list_projects, list_resources

projects = list_projects(url, token)
resources = list_resources(url, token, "my-project")

Or from the command line:

armadillo-flwr-resources

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Flower's open-source edition has no built-in authentication. This package adds OIDC token routing so each client node can authenticate with its local Armadillo server.

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
Researcher                 Flower                   Container
    |                         |                         |
    | authenticate            |                         |
    | (OIDC per URL)          |                         |
    |                         |                         |
    | armadillo-flwr-run      |                         |
    | (inject tokens) ------->|                         |
    |                         |                         |
    |                    ServerApp                      |
    |                    extract_tokens()               |
    |                    forward via ConfigRecord        |
    |                         |                         |
    |                         |------------------------>|
    |                         |                  get_node_url()
    |                         |                  (reads ARMADILLO_URL env)
    |                         |                  get_node_token(msg)
    |                         |                  (matches by sanitized URL)
    |                         |                         |

docs.title: URL Sanitization
docs.text:
Each Armadillo URL is converted to a config-safe key using sanitize_url():

URL Sanitized Key https://armadillo-demo.molgenis.net armadillo-demo-molgenis-net http://localhost:8080 localhost-8080 https://armadillo.dev.molgenis.org/ armadillo-dev-molgenis-org

The sanitization:

  1. Lowercases the URL
  2. Strips the scheme (https://)
  3. Strips trailing slashes
  4. Replaces non-alphanumeric characters with hyphens
  5. Collapses consecutive hyphens

This ensures https://Demo.Molgenis.NET/ and http://demo.molgenis.net produce the same key.

docs.title: Token File Format
docs.text:
After authentication, tokens are saved as:

{
  "token-armadillo-demo-molgenis-net": "eyJ...",
  "url-armadillo-demo-molgenis-net": "https://armadillo-demo.molgenis.net"
}

The token-* keys are injected into Flower's --run-config. The url-* keys are kept locally for the armadillo-flwr-resources CLI.

docs.title: Environment Variable
docs.text: Armadillo injects the ARMADILLO_URL environment variable into each container it starts. The helper functions read this to identify which token belongs to the current node — no manual configuration needed on the container side.
